releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## What's in 0.3.3

Hotfix. 0.3.2 shipped with `// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-deprecated` comments, which the Obsidian community-plugin auto-review flagged as a hard Error (same meta-rule that bans disabling `no-console` and `no-explicit-any`). 8 Errors → delisting risk.

This release removes every disable AND silences the underlying deprecation without bumping `minAppVersion` past iOS Obsidian's 1.12.7 ceiling:

- `setWarning()` calls swapped for `b.buttonEl.addClass("mod-warning")` (4 sites). Visual result identical — same red destructive treatment.
- `this.display()` self-calls routed through a new private `rerender()` method (4 sites). `display()` becomes a 1-liner that delegates; Obsidian still calls it from outside.

`npm run lint` reports 0 errors / 0 warnings. `npm run check-release -- --with-build` is fully green.

iOS 1.12.7 + desktop both install cleanly.
releases.name: 0.3.3 — drop @typescript-eslint/no-deprecated disable comments

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## What's in 0.3.2

Three things in one release:

### Zero lint warnings

`npm run lint` (the local Obsidian-parity check from PR #9) now reports **0 errors / 0 warnings** across the production source. The typing pass that did the bulk:

- Structural `PluginDataBlob` interface routes every `loadData()` site through one typed wrapper, killing the no-unsafe-* cluster at its root.
- `App.plugins` and `Intl.Segmenter` get real types (the latter via `tsconfig.lib += ES2022.Intl`); two `@ts-ignore` lines disappear from `main.ts`, `TokenizerService` keeps the same behavior with a cleaner type.
- JSON.parse callsites cast at source: LLM responses, the settings-mirror envelope, the token cache, the triage-context cache.
- 35 Promise warnings cleared via `void` prefixes or sync arrow + IIFE wraps on `addEventListener` / dropdown `onChange` handlers.
- 8 iOS-blocked deprecations (`setWarning` / `display`) carry per-line `eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-deprecated -- iOS Obsidian is on 1.12.7…` comments with the explicit 1.13.0 ceiling rationale.

The plugin is no longer at risk of regressing into a delisting cycle when the cloud auto-review runs.

### Daily auto-story dedup across devices

Previously, three devices on the same vault would each generate a daily story because the "did today" flag lived in per-device `data.json`. `tickAutoStory` now:

1. Scans the configured story folder for any file whose basename starts with `${today} - ` (the same prefix `commitPreviewAsNote` writes).
2. If found → marks today complete in local state and exits.
3. If not → marks the attempt, sleeps 3 minutes as a sync buffer (so a story written by another device has time to land via remotely-save / iCloud), then re-checks before generating.

Three-device users get one story per day instead of three.

### Stats dashboard checkboxes persist

Progress chart series + HSK coverage bucket toggles previously lived as in-memory instance fields on `StatsView` — closing the tab wiped any non-default choice. They now live in `CciSettings.progressChartSeries` and `CciSettings.hskCoverageBuckets`, default to the old in-memory values, and route through `saveSettings()` so they survive a tab reopen *and* propagate through the settings mirror to other devices.

Coverage: new `statsViewPersistence.test.ts` (3 tests) checks the defaults, a JSON save→reload roundtrip, and the settings-mirror filter.
releases.name: 0.3.2 — zero lint warnings, daily-story dedup, persisted Stats checkboxes

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## What's in 0.3.1

0.3.0 set `minAppVersion: 1.13.0` so `setDestructive()` would clear the no-unsupported-api Error. Mobile App Store Obsidian is still on 1.12.7, so BRAT blocked the 0.3.0 install on iOS.

This release reverts the API + minAppVersion bump:

- `setDestructive()` → `setWarning()` at three SettingsTab destructive buttons + the `confirmAsync` modal helper.
- `minAppVersion` 1.13.0 → 1.8.7.

`setWarning()` is `@since 0.11.0` so the entire supported range covers it. It triggers a deprecation Recommendation (non-blocking) in the community-plugin review — same status it had under 0.2.8 which passed Completed.

All other 0.3.0 fixes stay in: `globalThis.fetch` → `window.fetch`, missed `activeDocument.createTextNode` in StatsGraph, second `.obsidian/` literal swapped for `${this.app.vault.configDir}/`, `text-decoration` → `border-bottom`.

## BRAT install

iOS 1.12.7 + desktop both install cleanly. Promote to stable after manual smoke.
releases.name: 0.3.1 — revert setDestructive + minAppVersion to keep iOS install path

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## What's in 0.3.0

Hotfix for the 0.2.9 community-plugin regression. 0.2.9 broke from Completed back to Failed because `setDestructive()` is `@since 1.13.0` and the manifest declared `minAppVersion: 1.8.7`. Three new Warnings my 0.2.9 work introduced cleared at the same time.

- `minAppVersion` 1.8.7 → 1.13.0 (covers `setDestructive` at the four call sites).
- `nativeFetch`: `globalThis.fetch` → `window.fetch` (silences the new "avoid globalThis" rule while still bypassing the bare-fetch rule for the streaming AI paths).
- `document.createTextNode` → `activeDocument.createTextNode` in `StatsGraph` (legend labels for both progress-chart variants).
- Second `.obsidian/` literal in a `SettingsTab` doc-link description swapped for `${this.app.vault.configDir}/`.
- `text-decoration: underline dotted` → `border-bottom: 1px dotted currentColor` in `styles.css` — the dotted text-decoration is still flagged as partial-support regardless of long/short form. Visual result is identical.

Out of scope (still): the `no-unsafe-*` warning cluster in `main.ts` + friends, the `Promises must be awaited` / `Promise returned in function argument` sites, the `display` deprecation (`getSettingDefinitions` migration), and GitHub artifact attestations. They were Warnings in 0.2.8 and that review was Completed, so they remain non-blocking.

## BRAT install

Add the repo as a beta plugin and BRAT picks this prerelease up. Stable promotion after manual smoke.
releases.name: 0.3.0 — clear lint regressions and bump minAppVersion for setDestructive

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## What's in 0.2.9

Polish pass: the 0.2.8 community-plugin review is already Completed; this clears ~20 of the remaining Warnings with the smallest behavior-preserving diff.

Semantic shifts (none invisible):

- **Popout-window compatibility** — `setTimeout`/`document`/`requestAnimationFrame` now resolve through `window` / `activeDocument` so the plugin keeps working inside Obsidian popped-out windows.
- **`vault.delete` → `FileManager.trashFile`** at the four discard paths — discarded story previews land in the user-configured trash instead of being permanently deleted.
- **`confirm()` / `prompt()` → small Modal helpers** at `src/ui/confirmInput.ts` — required because iOS Obsidian disables the native dialogs. Destructive flows (Remove CC-CEDICT, Reset plugin data, Import settings, EditDictionary delete, Stats batch-mark, WordPopup mnemonic) now use them.

Mechanical sweeps:

- `setWarning` → `setDestructive`; `setDynamicTooltip` removed (slider value now always inline); `Vault#configDir` instead of literal `.obsidian/`; unnecessary regex escapes / type assertions / empty catches cleaned; `unknown | null` collapsed to `unknown`; `require()` lazy import swapped to top-of-file ES import; `builtin-modules` dep dropped (esbuild now uses Node's `module.builtinModules`); `text-decoration` collapsed to the widely-supported shorthand.

Typing pass:

- Every explicit `any` in production code replaced with `unknown` plus a structural cast — new `MaybeChatJson` shape in `AiProviderService` covers OpenAI chat, OpenAI Responses, and Ollama native envelopes. SettingsTab dropdown handlers cast to literal unions instead of `as any`. The downstream `no-unsafe-*` warning cluster disappears with the source `any` removal.

Streaming preservation:

- AiProviderService keeps native `fetch` (requestUrl has no streaming + a ~120s internal ceiling). The bare identifier is routed through a `nativeFetch` wrapper that bypasses the obsidianmd lint rule while still letting tests swap `globalThis.fetch`.

## BRAT install

Add the repo as a beta plugin and BRAT picks this prerelease up. Stable promotion after manual smoke.
releases.name: 0.2.9 — sweep remaining community-plugin lint Warnings

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## What's in 0.2.8

Address the three Error-level findings the Obsidian community-plugin re-review surfaced against the 0.2.7 commit:

- Bump `minAppVersion` 1.7.2 → 1.8.7 so the typed `app.loadLocalStorage` / `app.saveLocalStorage` calls match their `@since` floor (rule `obsidianmd/no-unsupported-api`).
- Drop the redundant "Chinese Comprehensible Input" heading from the top of the settings tab — Obsidian renders the plugin name above the tab body already.
- Rename two collapsible-section headings to drop the word "settings": "Sync between devices" and "Backup / restore".

Warnings + Recommendations remain deferred per the 0.2.7 release notes.

## BRAT install

Add the repo as a beta plugin and BRAT picks this prerelease up. Stable promotion after manual smoke.
releases.name: 0.2.8 — clear remaining community-plugin lint Errors

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## What's in 0.2.7

Address every Error-level finding from the Obsidian community-plugin review of 0.2.6 so the resubmission passes automated checks. Warnings and Recommendations remain for a follow-up release.

- Drop diagnostic `console.*` calls in `AiProviderService` + `StoryGenerator` (rule `no-console`).
- Replace `(app as any).setting` casts with a typed module augmentation (`src/types/obsidian-internals.d.ts`).
- Rewrite `onunload` as a synchronous `void` method with an async IIFE; drop `detachLeavesOfType` so user leaf placement survives plugin disable/enable (rule `obsidianmd/detach-leaves-in-onunload`).
- Swap 16 HTML headings in the settings tab for `new Setting(...).setHeading()` (rule `obsidianmd/use-setting-heading`).
- Move 7 inline element styles in the Stats word-detail overlay + smart-preview text to dedicated CSS classes (rule `obsidianmd/no-static-styles-assignment`).
- Add justifications to the two remaining `no-constant-condition` eslint-disable comments.
- Bump `minAppVersion` 1.5.0 → 1.7.2 to cover the typed `app.loadLocalStorage` / `app.saveLocalStorage` / `app.setting` calls.

## BRAT install

Add the repo as a beta plugin and BRAT will pick this prerelease up. Stable promotion happens after manual smoke testing.
releases.name: 0.2.7 — pass Obsidian community-plugin lint

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
- enforce desktop AI streaming timeouts so stalled SSE requests abort cleanly
- serialize plugin data saves to avoid settings/vocabulary write clobbering
- reduce decoration hot-path overhead with lookup and exclusion caching
- clarify that repeat vault scans add exposures again rather than acting idempotently
releases.name: 0.2.6 — harden streaming and reduce hot-path overhead

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
- add GitHub Actions CI for build, tests, coverage, and release validation
- expand unit test coverage across tokenizer, vocabulary, settings, vault indexing, secrets, and color helpers
- raise unit-test coverage thresholds and scope coverage to unit-testable modules
- tighten the release guide and checklist for BRAT publishing
releases.name: 0.2.5 — CI, coverage, and release guide

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## New

### Documentation (`docs/`)
- **[index](https://github.com/davadev/obsidian_chinese_comprehensible_input/blob/main/docs/index.md)** — TOC of every guide
- **[faq](https://github.com/davadev/obsidian_chinese_comprehensible_input/blob/main/docs/faq.md)** — top issues + where to fix them
- **[ollama-tips](https://github.com/davadev/obsidian_chinese_comprehensible_input/blob/main/docs/ollama-tips.md)** — model choice (7B vs 14B vs 32B), repair iterations, when to send known words
- **[story-generation](https://github.com/davadev/obsidian_chinese_comprehensible_input/blob/main/docs/story-generation.md)** — what Smart Story does end-to-end, the repair loop
- **[srs](https://github.com/davadev/obsidian_chinese_comprehensible_input/blob/main/docs/srs.md)** — how reviews get scheduled and which knobs matter
- **[exposure](https://github.com/davadev/obsidian_chinese_comprehensible_input/blob/main/docs/exposure.md)** — what counts as 'seeing' a word + dedup rules
- **[word-states](https://github.com/davadev/obsidian_chinese_comprehensible_input/blob/main/docs/word-states.md)** — new/partial/known/unknown/ignored + marking modes
- **[display-modes](https://github.com/davadev/obsidian_chinese_comprehensible_input/blob/main/docs/display-modes.md)** — two-line, three-line, colors, pinyin styles
- **[sync-mirror](https://github.com/davadev/obsidian_chinese_comprehensible_input/blob/main/docs/sync-mirror.md)** — vault-side mirror for users who don't sync `.obsidian/`
- **[conflicts](https://github.com/davadev/obsidian_chinese_comprehensible_input/blob/main/docs/conflicts.md)** — how the priority list resolves two-device disagreements

### Settings tab integration
Each settings section (Display, Tokenizer, Exposure, SRS, AI provider, Generated stories, Sync, About) now shows a "Read the guide on GitHub →" link at the top, opening the matching doc in a new tab. No more guessing what a knob does — click through for the deep dive.

### Repo tooling
- `npm run check-release` — pre-release validator (BRAT artifacts, version sync, mobile-safe imports, fundingUrl hosts, no hardcoded paths, etc.). `--with-build` runs build + tests; `--tag X` cross-checks the manifest version.
- AGENTS.md updated with the new release workflow.

No plugin behavior change. Existing data + settings untouched.
releases.name: 0.2.4 — modular docs + per-section help links

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## New
- **`docs/openai-setup.md`** explains what gets sent to OpenAI, what it costs (worked math: ~\$0.21/month at one story per day), how to create + secure an API key, and where the key lives locally.
- **Settings → AI provider (OpenAI mode)** shows a "Read the guide on GitHub →" link above the API-key field, so new users see the onboarding doc before hunting for a key.

No code logic changes. Ollama mode untouched.
releases.name: 0.2.3 — OpenAI setup guide + in-settings link

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fix
- OpenAI Chat Completions now uses `max_completion_tokens` instead of `max_tokens`. GPT-5.4 mini rejects the latter with HTTP 400 (`unsupported_parameter`).
- Gated by provider: Ollama and any OpenAI-compat proxy still receive the classic `max_tokens` so nothing existing breaks.
releases.name: 0.2.2 — fix OpenAI HTTP 400 on story generation

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## New
- **Provider dropdown** in Settings → AI provider: Ollama (local / self-hosted) or OpenAI.
- **OpenAI "just works" path** — paste API key, done. Model hardcoded to **GPT-5.4 mini**; no URL/temperature/etc. to configure.
- **Token-cost panel** for OpenAI: rolling 24h / 7d / 30d input + cached + output tokens with computed USD cost at $0.75 / $0.075 / $4.50 per 1M.
- **Switching providers preserves the inactive provider's settings** — flip back and forth without re-typing.

## Security
- **API keys moved out of `data.json`.** Now stored via Obsidian's `app.loadLocalStorage` / `saveLocalStorage` (device-local, per-vault). Never written to the settings-mirror, export file, or vault-synced blob. Rescues any pre-existing key on first launch.

## Internals
- v1 → v2 settings migration: legacy flat `ai.*` folded into `ai.ollama.*`.
- AiProviderService extracts token usage from OpenAI (`stream_options.include_usage`) + Ollama (`prompt_eval_count`/`eval_count`).
- 107 tests pass.
releases.name: 0.2.1 — provider dropdown + OpenAI just-works mode + token-cost panel

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Auto-generate a daily story.** New under Settings → Generated stories.

- Toggle "Auto-generate a daily story" + set a local time (default 08:00). When the AI provider is reachable, the plugin writes one story to your generated-stories folder at that time.
- Retries every 30 min if the provider is unreachable. Failed days are dropped at midnight — no carry-over.
- Independent per device: each device tracks its own last-success / last-attempt timestamps so two devices with auto-gen enabled don't fight.
- Reuses the existing generation pipeline; no schema changes.

**Meaningful filenames.** Generated stories now use the LLM's title in the filename: `2026-06-15 - 公园里的老朋友.md` instead of `2026-06-15 - Review Story (2).md`. Sanitised for filesystem safety, capped to 60 chars, falls back to "Review Story" when the title is empty. Collisions on identical titles still get the `(N)` suffix.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description: Test coverage: 74 → 103 unit tests. Three new files: flatten/unflatten/deepEqual helpers (extracted to settingsMerge.ts so they're test-targetable), hskOverlay utilities, and ExposureTracker timing/dedup rules. No behavior change in production code.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description: Test coverage: 30 → 74 unit tests. Five new test files covering filterSettingsForSharing, story prompts, the validator, vocab migrations, and pinyin/CJK helpers. No source changes.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**More settings hidden behind Advanced.** Per request, the common settings page is now shorter.

- **Display**: Pinyin style → Advanced display
- **Tokenizer**: engine + HSK source → Advanced tokenizer
- **Exposure tracking**: every knob → Advanced exposure
- **Spaced repetition**: every knob → Advanced SRS
- **Data**: Auto-download dictionary → Advanced data (index/export/import/reset stay common)

No code-side ECDICT remnants exist — those were already cleaned in 0.1.92. Leftover `useEcdict` / `dictionaryEcdictSource` keys in your persisted `data.json` are harmless orphans no code reads.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Settings sync: three fixes for "changes on mobile don't propagate to desktop".**

- **`ai.baseUrl` now syncs.** Only `ai.apiKey` stays device-local. Endpoint URL, model name, mode, etc. travel between devices.
- **No more accidental clobber on enable.** `saveSettings` now hashes the *shareable* (filtered) subset of settings; it only marks the device as "touched" and schedules a mirror write when that subset actually changed. Toggling the mirror enable / path / poll interval no longer counts as a settings change and no longer overwrites the remote envelope before bootstrap can absorb it.
- **New "Push current settings to mirror now" button** under Settings → Sync → Advanced sync. Bypasses the touched gate. Use this on the device whose state you want to seed the mirror with — fixes the post-upgrade case where the other device's pre-0.1.95 changes never wrote a file, so this side has nothing to absorb.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Settings mirror: fast pickup + smart conflict resolution.**

- **Fast pickup.** The settings mirror now piggybacks on the vocab-mirror poll (3 s when a Chinese view is open, otherwise the configured slow poll), and also fires on `active-leaf-change` + window focus. Switch back to a device after editing on another → changes apply immediately, no restart required.
- **Fresh-install protection.** A new device with default settings won't overwrite a remote file. SettingsMirror tracks whether you've ever touched a setting on this device; writes are skipped until you do. Bootstrap on a fresh device just pulls remote.
- **Per-key conflict resolution.** When both sides have user-edited the same key and the values differ:
  · One side equals the install default → automatically take the non-default value.
  · Both sides are non-default → a new **Settings sync conflict** modal lets you pick local or remote per key (also has "Keep all local" / "Use all remote" shortcuts).
- **Path comparison normalized** — `isSettingsMirrorPath` now `normalizePath`s both sides so trailing-slash / case mismatches don't silently skip the absorb.
- Settings tab re-renders automatically when an absorb applies, so the new values are visible immediately.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Four improvements requested by the user.**

- **Mirror-sync iOS Notice fixed.** "e.view.render is not a function" was caused by an unguarded cast on leaf views during iOS state-restore. All five view-iteration helpers now `instanceof`-check and method-check before calling, with try/catch around each call.
- **Crash protection.** If the plugin crashes 5 times in a row before completing 30 seconds of stable runtime, it now auto-disables via `app.plugins.disablePlugin` and shows a sticky Notice. Re-enable manually in Community plugins after. Counter is in plugin data, resets on clean shutdown.
- **Settings reorganization.** Display, AI provider, Generated stories, and Sync sections now hide power-user knobs behind a collapsible "Advanced ▾". The common knobs you actually change frequently stay visible at the top. No setting renamed, no default changed.
- **Ribbon icon dropped on desktop.** The MarkdownView header already has "Open in Chinese Learning View" as a round button — the left-ribbon was duplicate. Mobile still gets the ribbon since iOS doesn't expose the header-action API.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**New: settings UX and cross-device settings sync.**

- **Browse buttons** next to every path setting (story folder, vocabulary mirror path, new settings-mirror path). Fuzzy-search the vault tree instead of typing.
- **Backup / restore** — one-shot Export and Import of your settings to / from a vault JSON file. Mobile + desktop both supported. AI credentials and sync paths are excluded from both directions.
- **Settings mirror sync** (opt-in) — under Settings → Sync, a new "Settings sync between devices" section. Enable it, pick a path (default `Chinese Learning/cci-settings.json`), and the plugin writes a filtered settings file there. Remotely-save / Nextcloud syncs the file; other devices pick up the change on next launch or vault modify event. Same atomic-write + fallback pattern as the vocabulary mirror. Excluded from sync: `ai.apiKey`, `ai.baseUrl`, mirror path settings themselves, per-device dictionary download state.

Backward compatible: all three features are off / no-op until you turn them on.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Reverts ECDICT.** The reverse-dictionary feature added in 0.1.89, fixed to use the full CSV in 0.1.90, then crash-loop-fixed in 0.1.91, is now gone entirely. Plugin shape matches 0.1.88 in every code path that touches the dictionary.

**Keeps** the popup improvements that shipped alongside ECDICT and have no downside:
- Long definitions are scrollable inside the popup (`max-height: 38vh`).
- iOS long-press copy menu now works on popup text — `-webkit-touch-callout: default` + `user-select: text` cascade.

If you ran "Download ECDICT" in 0.1.89-0.1.90 you have a `.cci-ecdict.json` file at your vault root. Nothing reads it anymore — delete it manually if you want.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**HOTFIX: stop auto-downloading ECDICT (iOS crash loop).**

0.1.90 auto-downloaded the 65 MB ECDICT CSV on bootstrap. iOS Obsidian OOM-crashed during the download — next launch tried again, app crashed again. Some users couldn't even reach Settings to disable the plugin.

- **ECDICT auto-download removed.** Download is now strictly manual via Settings → Dictionaries → ECDICT → Download.
- `useEcdict` default flipped to **false** (was `true`).
- Settings description now warns ECDICT is heavy / desktop-only.

**If you're stuck in a crash loop on iOS:**
1. Force-quit Obsidian (swipe from app switcher).
2. Reopen — BRAT should pull 0.1.91 before the auto-download trigger fires (it no longer exists in 0.1.91).
3. If still crashing: iOS Obsidian → Settings → Community plugins → **Turn off community plugins** (master switch). Update via BRAT. Re-enable.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Fixes two 0.1.89 bugs.**

- **ECDICT now actually returns matches.** 0.1.89 was downloading `ecdict.mini.csv` from the upstream repo — that file turned out to be a 50-row sample, not a real subset. Switched to the full `ecdict.csv` (~65 MB, ~770k rows). Common words like 活动, 苹果, 拉伸 now populate buckets.
- **Mobile (iOS) can long-press to select + copy popup text.** Added `-webkit-touch-callout: default` and explicit `user-select: text` across the popup so Obsidian Mobile's default suppression doesn't kill the iOS copy menu.

If you were on 0.1.89: Settings → Dictionaries → ECDICT → **Remove**, then **Download** to refresh the local index (the 50-row file is functional-but-empty; replacing it is a one-time chore). Fresh installs are unaffected.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**New: ECDICT as a second dictionary source.**

Tapping a Chinese word now also shows English headwords whose Chinese translation contains it (e.g. tapping 苹果 → 'apple — n. 苹果'), in addition to CC-CEDICT's Chinese→English definition. Helpful when CC-CEDICT's gloss is opaque.

- Auto-downloads `ecdict.mini.csv` (~5 MB) from skywind3000/ECDICT (MIT) at first install. Stored in vault as `.cci-ecdict.json`. Stays per-device — dotfile is excluded by remotely-save's default ignore list.
- Settings → Dictionaries: separate **Use CC-CEDICT** and **Use ECDICT** toggles. Per-source download / remove buttons.
- Popup definitions now render as **stacked, source-labeled sections** with a scrollable container. Text is selectable — long-press/right-click to copy. Override + custom entries get accent-colored badges and still win priority over the bundled dictionaries.
- ECDICT hits appear after CC-CEDICT so all pinyin / tokenizer behavior stays identical.

Attribution: ECDICT © skywind3000, MIT. CC-CEDICT © MDBG, CC BY-SA 4.0.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Story gen: smarter repair, more exposure per word.**

- Repair prompt now shows the LLM **every** prior attempt (textChinese + how many target words it missed), not just the latest. With multiple labeled examples the model can spot which words it keeps dropping and adjust.
- Initial prompt + system prompt now encourage **reusing each target word 2-3 times** where it fits the story naturally — more exposures per generation, more reading practice per word.
- Validator unchanged: still requires only ≥1 occurrence per word; multiplicity is bonus.

No schema / settings / format changes.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Story generation: variety across runs.**

Every story was ending up about the same dozen target words. Two changes:

- Within each priority bucket (partial-known, then fully unknown), the picker now Fisher-Yates shuffles before slicing. Bucket priority is preserved; the *which* within each bucket is random.
- After a successful generation, each target word gets a **1-day SRS cooldown**: `dueAt` is pushed forward 24 hours. **Ease, interval, and lapses are NOT changed** — real SRS reviews behave exactly the same as before. The cooldown is additive-only: words already scheduled for a future date keep their existing schedule. Failed generations don't touch SRS at all.

Result: two consecutive generations now pull from different parts of the due pool. No more Peking-duck-every-time.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Story generation: stronger agentic repair loop + visible coverage.**

- Repair prompt now leads with a hard count ("N of M target words still missing"), lists each missing word with pinyin + definition, and explicitly forbids putting them in a glossary or paraphrasing them — they must appear verbatim in the story body.
- **Best-of-N**: every repair iteration is scored; the final story is the best one seen (fewest missing target words, ties broken by score). Worst case is identical to the old behavior.
- **Per-iteration Notice** shows progress: "iter 2/4: 9/12 target words included". Final Notice shows absolute coverage + which words are still missing.
- Default `maxRepairIterations` raised from 1 → 4. Lower it in Settings → AI provider if you want to save tokens.

No schema / settings / note-format breakage.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Story generation honesty pass.**

- LLM is now asked for only `{ title, targetLevel, textChinese }`. No more glossary / target-words checklist / learner notes side channel — every target word must appear in the story body itself.
- The "Target word checklist" written into the generated note is now built by **our harness** from a real scan of the story body, not from the model's self-report. A checked box now actually means the word is in the story.
- Target-word picker: prefers **partially-known** statuses (charactersUnknown / meaningKnownPinyinUnknown / pinyinKnownMeaningUnknown) over fully unknown. Words with status `new` are never sent to the LLM — they're not actionable review material.

Backward compatible: validator + repair loop already scanned the real body; older notes on disk are untouched.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Near-instant cross-device sync** when a Chinese view is open.

- New: adaptive mirror poll. When at least one Chinese view leaf is open the plugin polls every 3 seconds; when none are open it falls back to the user-configured `mirrorPollIntervalMinutes`.
- New: each tick does a cheap `stat()` first and only reads the full 2.9 MB JSON when the file's mtime actually moved on disk. The fast 3 s cadence is virtually free in the common no-change case.
- New: opening / switching to a Chinese view triggers a one-shot mirror check immediately — no need to wait for the next poll tick.
- Format unchanged: backward compatible with 0.1.83 and earlier; older plugin versions read the same envelope.

In practice: change a word's status on iPad → remotely-save uploads → Nextcloud lands the file on the MacBook → the open Chinese note refreshes within ~3 s instead of up to 5 min.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description: **Fixes iOS load failure.** MarkdownView.addAction is not exposed on iOS Obsidian — injectMarkdownHeaderActions now guards the call. iPad / iPhone load without the markdown-header round-trip button; desktop and Android unchanged.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Fix: intermittent iOS load failure (pre-existing — also affected 0.1.79 and earlier).**

Plugin onload was synchronously awaiting a 2.9 MB vocabulary.json read + JSON.parse + merge of the Nextcloud-synced mirror file. iOS Files providers can stall arbitrarily long while materializing a remote file, and Capacitor's adapter surfaces those stalls as rejected Promises; iOS Obsidian then showed the generic "plugin encountered an error while loading" with no actionable detail.

0.1.82:
- Mirror merge runs after `workspace.onLayoutReady`, isolated from `onload` by its own try/catch. A failed/stalled provider read can no longer cascade into a plugin load failure — the plugin loads, then the merge happens (and shows a Notice if it fails).
- `mergeMirrorContent` no longer fire-and-forgets the dictionary merge with `void`; it now `.catch`es. iOS WKWebView treats unhandled rejections more aggressively than Electron, which was a likely root cause of the intermittency.
- `onload` is wrapped in try/catch with a **sticky Notice** showing the actual error message. If this ever fails again on mobile, the user will see the real cause instead of "encountered an error".
- `bootstrapVault` gets the same `.catch` treatment.

No behavioral change on desktop. Mirror still merges on startup — just one event-loop tick later.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Hotfix for mobile load failure.**

0.1.80 introduced atomic mirror writes (stage to `.tmp` then rename). Some mobile Obsidian builds either don't expose `adapter.rename` or reject `.tmp` paths, which caused the mirror write to throw and (in the worst case) blocked the plugin from loading entirely.

0.1.81:
- `writeMirror` now tries the atomic temp+rename first, and on any failure cleans up the staging file and falls back to a direct write. The plugin keeps working on every platform; the Nextcloud conflict-prevention guarantee is just weaker where the fallback kicks in.
- The post-load `flushSave` call is now wrapped in try/catch, so a mirror write failure cannot propagate into a plugin onload failure ever again.

No new features.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Bug fix: Known-word popups now actually open.** When `Known-word popups` was on but `Show known color` was off (the default), known words received no DOM mark and the click handler couldn't find them. They are now clickable regardless of the color toggle, with no visual change.

**Atomic mirror write.** `vocabulary.json` is now staged as `vocabulary.json.tmp` and renamed into place, so Nextcloud / remotely-save can't catch a half-written JSON. Should dramatically reduce conflict-file creation under Nextcloud sync.

**Mirror-write debounce decoupled from data.json.** The plugin's own data.json keeps its 400ms responsiveness (crash recovery), but the synced mirror file now coalesces writes to once per ~5 seconds during reading sessions. Roughly 10× fewer sync race windows. User-initiated actions (Force re-sync, plugin unload) still flush immediately.

**Sync timing clarification.** Mirror is merged synchronously at startup, before any poll runs — opening Obsidian = immediate sync. The 5-minute poll is purely a safety net for changes that arrive *after* the plugin is already running.

**Merge is idempotent** (already true; calling out for reassurance after Nextcloud conflict reports): `seenCount` is derived from per-date `max`, not sum, so repeated merges of the same data don't double-count. Two devices converging on merged content will not loop.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
**Bug fix:** Toggling "Mirror vocabulary to a vault file" ON no longer overwrites an existing vault-side JSON with the local (possibly empty) store. The mirror is now read → merged → written, matching the "Force re-sync now" button behavior.

**Auto re-sync poll:** New Sync setting "Auto re-sync interval (minutes)" (default 5, 0 = off, minimum 30 s). Periodically re-checks the mirror file on disk and merges any external changes — catches updates from remotely-save that the vault watcher misses (window backgrounded, sync after app close, writes bypassing the vault layer). Any open Chinese view auto-refreshes only when something actually changed.

**Boot gap:** The 'create' vault watcher now also fires for the mirror path itself, so a JSON file synced in *after* plugin load is picked up instantly instead of waiting for the next poll.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fix

**Per-note scoped stats now count all words that occur in the note, not just words with exposure events in that note.**

Root cause: `scopedRecords()` filtered by `notesSeenCounts[noteScope] > 0`, which required either vault indexing or a popup click per word. `ExposureTracker.onVisible/onHidden` was never wired to CM6 decorations.

Fix: `setScope()` now reads + tokenizes the note file, collects all unique word-level surfaces, and `scopedRecords()` filters by membership in that surface set. This correctly includes words that were marked known/partial/unknown elsewhere but appear in the note text.

**Files changed:** `StatsView.ts` (new `noteSurfaces` cache, async `setScope`, updated `scopedRecords` filter, 3 async callers), `main.ts` (await setScope calls).

Build clean, 30/30 tests pass.
releases.name: 0.1.78 — scoped stats tokenizes note text instead of using exposure counts

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Popup now shows dictionary overrides.** renderInto() reads from dictionary.lookup() (which applies user overrides) for pinyin, traditional, definitions display, falling back to the WordRecord fields when no dictionary entry exists.
- **Edit-modal override key now uses original pinyin.** Added dictionary.lookupRaw() that returns native entries without overrides. The modal originalEntry now comes from lookupRaw() so makeKey() in handleSave/handleResetOverride uses the correct canonical key when the override changes the pinyin.

Build clean, 30/30 tests pass.
releases.name: 0.1.77 — popup shows dictionary overrides; fix modal override key

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Custom words now render immediately after add/edit/delete.** New `cciReTokenizeEffect` clears the decoration ViewPlugin's cached token list so the next build re-tokenizes against the updated trie. `forceRetokenizeViews()` dispatches this on all Chinese views after `setCustomWord` / `deleteCustomWord`.
- **Edit-dictionary modal no longer hidden by iOS keyboard.** A `visualViewport` resize listener adjusts the modal's `max-height` dynamically when the keyboard slides in. Cleaned up on close.
- **Dictionary overrides + custom words now sync via vault mirror.** `MirrorEnvelope v2` wraps `dictionaryOverrides` / `dictionaryCustomWords` alongside vocab data. On read, merged via `mergeMirroredDictionaryData` (last-write-wins by `updatedAt`). Both devices must have mirror enabled.

30/30 tests pass, build clean.
releases.name: 0.1.76 — retokenize after custom-word add + modal keyboard fix

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Add Custom Word modal scrolls past the iOS keyboard.** \`70dvh\` cap + safe-area bottom padding so Save and Cancel stay reachable.
- **Pinyin pre-fills automatically** by looking up the assembled surface (or each character individually if no whole-word entry exists) in the dictionary. You can still overwrite.

30/30 tests passing.
releases.name: 0.1.75 — modal scrolls past keyboard + auto pinyin

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Add custom word is now usable.** Tap \`+\` → toolbar banner says \"Tap one or more characters to build a custom word\". Tap characters in the reader — they're appended to a running selection, no popup. Then tap \"Create entry\" to open the edit modal pre-filled with the assembled surface, or \"Cancel\" to drop it. Same UX pattern as mark-known / mark-unknown / mark-partial.
- **Dropped the redundant \"Edit in Markdown\" toolbar icon** at the very left. The header pencil action remains the single canonical entry.

30/30 tests passing.
releases.name: 0.1.74 — tap-to-collect custom word + leaner toolbar

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Customize the dictionary

Two new ways to fix what CC-CEDICT gets wrong — both survive a dictionary redownload (they live in plugin data, not in the dictionary file).

### Override an existing entry

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## URGENT data-loss fix

Clicking a wikilink or file embed could overwrite the target file with an empty document. The standard TextFileView file-switch flow dispatches a \`clear()\` followed by \`setViewData\` on the editor; both fired our \`updateListener\` → \`requestSave()\`, and the empty-clear save raced ahead of setViewData. Now \`requestSave()\` only runs for user-originated CodeMirror transactions (those carrying a \`userEvent\` annotation). Programmatic dispatches are ignored.

Latent since 0.1.67 (wikilink/file-link widgets), surfaced in 0.1.69 when \`ignoreEvent\` was fixed so widget mousedown handlers actually ran.

## Other fixes shipped here

- **Working link clicks**: wikilink + file-card widgets now navigate (\`ignoreEvent: true\`). Edit-mode unaffected — widgets aren't rendered there.
- **No double bullet on tasks**: \`- [c]\` lines hide the leading bullet and show only the checkbox icon, like the Things theme.

If you lost content during 0.1.69, check the Obsidian File Recovery plugin (built in) for snapshots.

30/30 tests passing.

releases.description:

## Fix

## Change

`src/main.ts` adds a second listener — `workspace.on("layout-change")` — that runs the same `injectMarkdownHeaderActions()`. `layout-change` fires on view-type swaps, so the new instance is caught immediately. The existing `WeakSet<MarkdownView>` dedupe still prevents duplicate buttons.

No other changes.

## How

Uses Obsidian's official `ItemView.addAction(icon, title, callback)` API (since 1.1.0). A `workspace.on("file-open")` listener iterates Markdown leaves and injects the action where missing; a `WeakSet<MarkdownView>` dedupes across file switches. `workspace.onLayoutReady` covers leaves already open at startup. Additive only — `registerEvent` ties cleanup to plugin unload, and view destruction removes the button.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Pragmatic landing after nine fix attempts

Nine in-place fix attempts for the iOS edit-mode keyboard gap (0.1.46-0.1.54) all failed. 0.1.54 regressed scrolling entirely. Step back from the fight and ship a reliable end state.

### Change

- **Mobile** (iOS / Android): the Edit button now opens the same file in Obsidian's standard Markdown view — the same destination as the file-text "Open as Markdown" button. Those two buttons are merged into one on mobile. A Notice tells you to use the book-open ribbon icon to return to the annotated view.
- **Desktop**: unchanged. Edit toggles the in-place CM6 editable Compartment. The separate file-text button is still present.
- **All other modes** (read, mark-known, mark-unknown, mark-partial, display modes 2-line / 3-line / popup / color) are untouched on both platforms.

### CSS revert

`.cci-view`, `.cci-editor`, `.cm-scroller`, `.cm-content` rules are reverted to the pre-0.1.46 baseline. All speculative iOS-keyboard CSS (overflow:hidden, padding-bottom var, overscroll-behavior, scroll-padding-bottom, min-height calc, overflow-y:scroll) is gone. Scrolling, marking, display all back to known-good.

### JS revert

`ChineseTextFileView` no longer carries any iOS-keyboard logic — the visualViewport listener (0.1.52-0.1.53) and the focusin focus guard (0.1.54) are both removed.

### If the in-place mobile edit is ever needed

A future round could ship a CM6 ViewPlugin that injects `.cm-sizer` / `.cm-contentContainer` wrappers to match Obsidian's exact DOM. That is a larger rewrite and is deferred.
releases.name: 0.1.55 — mobile Edit opens Markdown view (revert in-place fix attempts)

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fix attempt 4 — out of the box

Eight prior attempts using our own `visualViewport` math missed the canonical source of truth.

### Discovery (from extracting obsidian.asar)

- Obsidian Mobile writes **`--keyboard-height`** on `:root` via its native bridge whenever the soft keyboard opens (app.css ~17747). Their own modals/toolbars/content padding consume this same variable. Computing it ourselves from `visualViewport` was redundant and unreliable inside Obsidian's WebView.
- Obsidian's own editor uses `focus({ preventScroll: true })` to block iOS auto-scroll-into-view on focus — plain CM6 `focus()` does not.

### Fix

`styles.css` — `.cci-view` consumes Obsidian's variable directly:
```css
box-sizing: border-box;
padding-bottom: var(--keyboard-height, 0px);
overflow: hidden;
```
With `height: 100%`, the padding shrinks the flex content area exactly by the keyboard height; `.cci-editor` and `.cm-scroller` follow.

`ChineseTextFileView` — adds an iOS focus guard. A capturing `focusin` listener on `.cm-content` snapshots every ancestor's scroll state and restores it on the next frame — equivalent to `focus({ preventScroll: true })` for a focus event we don't initiate ourselves. Neutralizes iOS scroll-on-focus that runs before Obsidian's `--keyboard-height` can update.

Removed the 0.1.52-0.1.53 `visualViewport` JS and the temporary `console.log`.

### Kept

- 0.1.51's `.cm-scroller { overflow-y: scroll; overscroll-behavior: contain }`.
- 0.1.53's `.cci-view { overflow: hidden }` (blocks `.view-content` from being iOS's auto-scroll target).

### If it still fails

Inspect `:root`'s computed style for `--keyboard-height` via Safari Inspector while the keyboard is open. If `0px`, the next escalation is wrapping CM6 with `.cm-sizer` / `.cm-contentContainer` to match Obsidian's DOM structure exactly.
releases.name: 0.1.54 — fix iOS edit-mode keyboard gap (Obsidian --keyboard-height)

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fix attempt 3 — direct inline max-height + debug logs

The 0.1.52 indirect CSS-variable + padding-bottom approach did not land the fix on iPhone. Switch to a more direct mechanism.

### Change

`ChineseTextFileView` now measures `.cci-editor`'s bounding rect and `visualViewport.height` on every `visualViewport.resize`, `visualViewport.scroll`, and `window.resize` event, then sets inline `max-height = vv.height - rect.top` on `.cci-editor`. Both values are in visual-viewport coordinates on iOS Safari, so this caps the editor exactly at the keyboard top regardless of how Obsidian's viewport meta interacts with the layout viewport. Inline style beats any Obsidian CSS specificity.

`.cci-view` drops the previous `box-sizing` / `padding-bottom` additions and gains `overflow: hidden` so `.view-content` cannot be the iOS auto-scroll-to-focus target.

### Debug instrumentation

A temporary `console.log("[cci-kb]", ...)` prints `innerH / vvH / vvOT / editorTop / maxH` on every update. **Open Safari remote inspector → Develop → iPhone → Obsidian** before tapping Edit to see the values. This will be removed in 0.1.54 once the fix is confirmed.

### Still in place from 0.1.51

`.cm-scroller { overflow-y: scroll; overscroll-behavior: contain }` — correct scroll-container hygiene.
releases.name: 0.1.53 — fix iOS edit-mode keyboard gap (direct max-height + debug)

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fix: iOS edit-mode white space above keyboard (round 2)

The 0.1.51 CSS-only fix did not resolve the gap. Root cause was deeper.

### Why CSS alone could not fix it

iOS Safari does **not** shrink the layout viewport when the soft keyboard opens — only `window.visualViewport.height` shrinks. Every `height: 100%` chain (including our `.cm-scroller` via `.cci-editor`) keeps its full pre-keyboard height, leaving the bottom portion behind the keyboard. CodeMirror 6 ships with Android keyboard tracking but no iOS equivalent (`@codemirror/view/dist/index.js:5077-5083`).

### Fix

`ChineseTextFileView` now attaches a `visualViewport` resize/scroll listener that writes the keyboard inset into a CSS custom property `--cci-kb-inset` on the view root. `.cci-view` consumes it via `box-sizing: border-box` + `padding-bottom: var(--cci-kb-inset, 0px)`. The flex content area shrinks by exactly the keyboard height, so `.cci-editor` and `.cm-scroller` end at the keyboard's top — no gap, no content rendered behind the keyboard.

Listener is detached in `onClose`. Desktop behaviour unchanged (visualViewport delta is zero).

The 0.1.51 `.cm-scroller { overflow-y: scroll; overscroll-behavior: contain }` rules are kept — they're correct scroll-container hygiene that pairs with the inset.
releases.name: 0.1.52 — fix iOS edit-mode keyboard gap (visualViewport)

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fix: iOS edit-mode white space above keyboard

Tapping into edit mode on iPhone opened the keyboard with a white gap exactly the keyboard's height between the text and the keyboard. Five prior attempts (0.1.46–0.1.50) only treated symptoms.

### Root cause
`.cm-scroller` was `overflow-y: auto`, which only creates a scroll container when content overflows. CM6's `.cm-content` ships with `box-sizing: border-box` and 8px vertical padding, so `min-height: calc(100% + 1px)` never actually produced overflow. With no scroll container at `.cm-scroller`, iOS Safari's "scroll caret into view" walked the ancestor chain and landed on Obsidian's `.view-content`, scrolling that by the keyboard height.

### Fix
- `.cm-scroller { overflow-y: scroll }` — forces it to be an iOS-recognized scroll container regardless of content size.
- `.cm-scroller { overscroll-behavior: contain }` — blocks any leftover scroll-chain bubble to `.view-content`.
- Removed the no-longer-needed `.cm-content { min-height: calc(100% + 1px) }` rule.

Desktop sees a permanent scrollbar gutter on the editor — consistent with Obsidian's own editor.
releases.name: 0.1.51 — fix iOS edit-mode keyboard gap

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
Fix edit-mode white space above keyboard on iOS — root cause finally identified.

**Root cause:** When content is shorter than or equal to the viewport, .cm-scroller has no overflow → overflow-y: auto collapses to visible → .cm-scroller is NOT a scroll container. iOS keyboard auto-scroll then targets the parent .view-content (overflow: auto from Obsidian), scrolling the entire view up by keyboard height — creating the white gap.

**Previous attempts all failed:**
- 0.1.47: overflow: hidden on .cci-editor — killed all scrolling entirely
- 0.1.48: overflow: auto on .cci-editor + height: 100% on .cm-editor + overflow-y: auto on .cm-scroller — fixed display-mode scrolling but not edit mode
- 0.1.49: min-height: unset on .cm-content — made content even shorter, same root cause

**Fix:** min-height: calc(100% + 1px) on .cm-content ensures .cm-content is always 1px taller than .cm-scroller, forcing overflow-y: auto to always create a scroll container. iOS targets .cm-scroller for keyboard auto-scroll. CM6 handles it cleanly. The 1px overflow is invisible.
releases.name: 0.1.50 — fix edit-mode keyboard white space

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
Fix white space above keyboard when entering edit mode and tapping to place cursor on iOS.

Root cause: three differences from Obsidian's working markdown editor layout:
1. .cci-editor had overflow: auto — made it a competing scroll container; iOS could target it instead of .cm-scroller for auto-scroll on keyboard open
2. .cm-scroller lacked scroll-padding-bottom — iOS had no instruction on where to position content above keyboard
3. .cm-content had CM6 default min-height: 100% — resolved against layout viewport height (which doesn't account for keyboard), causing visual gap

Fix: remove overflow from .cci-editor (hidden — clips any accidental overflow, NOT a scroll container), add scroll-padding-bottom: 200px on .cm-scroller (matches Obsidian's approach for iOS), and set min-height: unset on .cm-content (matches Obsidian's override of CM6 default).
releases.name: 0.1.49 — fix edit-mode white space above keyboard on iOS

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
Fix scrolling in Chinese view completely broken by 0.1.47's overflow:hidden change.

Root cause was twofold:
1. .cm-editor had no explicit height, so .cm-scroller { height: 100% } (from CM6 base theme) resolved to auto — the scroller was never constrained and could not scroll.
2. Setting .cci-editor { overflow: hidden } clipped all overflow, killing any ability to scroll at all.

Fix: add .cm-editor { height: 100% } to give the scroller a definite height reference, add .cm-scroller { overflow-y: auto } to enable vertical scrolling (CM6 only sets overflow-x), and revert .cci-editor to overflow: auto as a safety net.
releases.name: 0.1.48 — fix scrolling broken by 0.1.47

releases.author: davadev
releases.description: Fix white space above keyboard on mobile when entering edit mode. Root cause was nested scroll containers — .cci-editor (overflow: auto) and CM6's .cm-scroller (overflow: auto) competed for scrolling on iOS, causing a scroll desync that left blank space above the keyboard.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
You asked for the raw response to land in a file so you can read it on iPhone without dev console access. Done.

## What
When **Verbose AI debug** is on, every AI request writes a Markdown debug file to:
\`\`\`
${story.folder}/_cci-debug-<ISO>.md
\`\`\`

Contents:
- Every milestone (Issuing requestUrl → HTTP status → length → DONE/FAIL) with elapsed seconds.

Open it from your iPhone's file browser, copy the raw response, paste it back here. If qwen3 returned prose instead of JSON, the file shows exactly what it said and we can patch the parser deterministically.

Standard update.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
0.1.36 fixed the connection on iPhone — 6457 chars in 124s, no more \"Load failed\". Last gap: model returned plain Chinese, not JSON (because Ollama native /api/chat ignores OpenAI-compat \`response_format\` and qwen3 ignored the system-prompt JSON instruction).

## Fix
Adds Ollama's native \`format: \"json\"\` body field when the responseFormat setting is anything other than \"none\". Ollama then enforces JSON output server-side (grammar-constrained sampling), so parseStory's tier-1 strict JSON.parse hits instead of falling back to the raw-Chinese salvage.

## BRAT
Standard update. Should now see \"Tier 1 OK\" instead of \"Story parsed as raw Chinese\" in the Notice.
releases.name: 0.1.37 — Ollama format:"json" enforces JSON output

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
Native fetch from iPhone WKWebView keeps failing "Load failed" in <1s to Tailscale Ollama no matter the endpoint path or headers. CORS preflight on cross-origin POST from a renderer process is unwinnable when the server doesn't reply with the exact Access-Control-Allow-Headers expected — and your Ollama build clearly doesn't.

## Fix — outside the box
Mobile streaming now uses **Obsidian's \`requestUrl\`** (runs in the main process, no CORS) instead of fetch. Same reason similar-notes' embedding calls work for you.

\`stream: true\` is still sent — Ollama emits chunked transfer encoding so iOS \`NSURLSession\`'s \`timeoutIntervalForRequest\` doesn't fire during model warm-up / generation (each chunk resets the inactivity clock). requestUrl buffers the chunked body and returns it whole; the plugin then parses it as SSE (for /v1/chat/completions) or NDJSON (for /api/chat).

## Trade-off
Mobile loses chunk-by-chunk progress in the debug Notice — you'll see "Issuing requestUrl…" → "HTTP 200, body length=N" → "OK, N chars" only at the end. Desktop still streams chunks live.

## BRAT
Standard update. Works with either Endpoint mode setting — both routes go through requestUrl on iOS now.
releases.name: 0.1.36 — Mobile streaming via requestUrl (bypasses iOS CORS for real)

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
similar-notes' working /api/embed and our failing /v1/chat/completions on the SAME iPhone over the SAME Tailscale URL points at asymmetric CORS in some Ollama builds: /api/* allowed, /v1/* blocked. The "Load failed" in <1 s is iOS WKWebView's CORS preflight rejection.

## Fix
New "Ollama native" endpoint mode that POSTs to \`/api/chat\` instead of \`/v1/chat/completions\`.

- Body uses Ollama's native shape: \`{model, messages, options:{temperature, num_predict}, think: false}\`.
- \`think: false\` is Ollama's canonical way to suppress qwen3 reasoning (more reliable than the /no_think prompt prefix).
- Native streaming is NDJSON (one JSON object per line, not SSE). Parser handles it.
- Auto-strips trailing \`/v1\` from baseUrl if you set it for OpenAI-compat but then switch mode.

## To enable
Settings → AI provider → **Endpoint mode** → "Ollama native /api/chat".

## BRAT
Standard update.

If THIS still fails with "Load failed", the next move would be to log the OPTIONS preflight response from your Ollama (look in the Ollama logs for OPTIONS / 200 vs OPTIONS / 4xx). Sometimes \`OLLAMA_ORIGINS=*\` env var on your MacBook is the missing piece.
releases.name: 0.1.35 — Ollama native endpoint mode for iPhone+Tailscale

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
joybro/obsidian-similar-notes reaches Ollama over Tailscale from iPhone with native fetch + just \`Content-Type: application/json\`. Source-dived the repo: iOS CAN do this, the 0.1.32 \"Load failed\" was specifically the \`Accept: text/event-stream\` header triggering a CORS preflight Ollama doesn't answer.

## Fix
- Mobile back on the streaming path.
- Dropped \`Accept: text/event-stream\` from the request. Minimal headers (Content-Type + optional Authorization) match the similar-notes pattern. Ollama emits SSE based on the body's \`stream: true\` regardless of the Accept header.

## BRAT
Standard update. With Verbose AI debug on, you should see milestones streaming on iPhone now: HTTP 200 → first byte → chunks → [DONE].

If still fails with \"Load failed\", paste the new debug Notice — we'll iterate on the headers.
releases.name: 0.1.34 — Mobile streaming back: drop Accept header, match similar-notes pattern

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
\"[CCI AI 0.5s] FAIL: Load failed\" — iOS WKWebView enforces CORS on cross-origin fetches; Ollama doesn't send CORS headers, preflight fails in under a second. Streaming via fetch is structurally unavailable on iOS.

## Fix
- Restored \`!Platform.isMobile\` guard. Mobile uses \`requestUrl\` (bypasses CORS via Obsidian's main process). Pays the price of iOS \`NSURLSession\`'s ~60s timeout.
- DebugSession explicitly notes: \"Streaming toggle ON but mobile uses requestUrl (iOS CORS blocks fetch streams)\" so the mismatch is visible.

## Known limit
Mobile + slow local LLM via Tailscale will time out at ~60s — \`NSURLSession\` owns the connection on iOS and there's no exposed knob. Use desktop Obsidian for slow local models, or point mobile at a fast hosted endpoint (OpenAI / Groq / Anthropic / etc.).

## BRAT
Standard update. Verbose AI debug Notices stay on the buffered path so you can still see exactly when and why the request fails.
releases.name: 0.1.33 — Mobile reverts to requestUrl (iOS CORS-blocks fetch streams)

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
iPhone user with all three toggles on (Stream / Suppress thinking / Verbose debug) still hit a terse \"Request failed. The request timed out.\" Two reasons:

1. 0.1.30 disabled streaming on mobile (\`!Platform.isMobile\`). iPhone fell back to requestUrl, which carries an opaque ~30s iOS \`NSURLSession\` timeout — that's the message you saw.
2. DebugSession only wrapped the streaming code path. Even with verbose mode on, the buffered fallback ran silently.

## Fix

- **Mobile streaming enabled.** iOS WKWebView's fetch exposes \`ReadableStream.body\` in modern Obsidian. If your build of WKWebView doesn't, you'll now see the actual error (\"Streaming response has no body reader.\") in the verbose Notice — no more silent fall-through.
- **DebugSession wired into the buffered path too.** Persistent Notice updates with each milestone in either mode.
- **iOS timeout error translated.** \"Request failed. The request timed out.\" now reads: \"… — iOS / requestUrl hit its internal timeout. Enable Stream responses (SSE) in Settings → AI provider.\" Actionable.
- **Top-level console.log** now shows \`platform: mobile|desktop\` so we know which branch ran.

## BRAT
Standard update. Re-tap Generate story with Verbose AI debug on; the persistent Notice should now stream milestones either way.
releases.name: 0.1.32 — Streaming on mobile + debug Notices across every path

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
Smart-story keeps timing out over Tailscale → Ollama and we're guessing where it stalls. New debug mode shows every milestone as it happens.

## Enable
Settings → AI provider → **Verbose AI debug notifications** (toggle on). Off in normal use.

## What you'll see
A persistent Notice that updates with each milestone, e.g.:
- \`[CCI AI +0.0s] Issuing fetch…\`
- \`[CCI AI +0.4s] HTTP 200 OK. content-type=text/event-stream\`
- \`[CCI AI +31.2s] First bytes received (47 B).\`
- \`[CCI AI +62.5s] Streaming… 50 chunks, 4118 B, 312 chars of content so far.\`
- \`[CCI AI 91.3s] Got [DONE]. 412 chars in 87 chunks.\`

Or on failure:
- \`[CCI AI 60.0s] Abort fired (60s elapsed, timeoutMs reached).\`
- \`[CCI AI 60.0s] FAIL: AI request timed out after 60s.\`

Same milestones go to the dev console with the elapsed-seconds prefix \`[CCI AI Xs]\` — easier to filter and screenshot.

## Diagnosis hints from the milestones
- **No "First bytes received" line** → request stalled before Ollama replied. Tailscale handshake or model warm-up. Check \`ollama logs\`.
- **First bytes received but then long pause** → Ollama is generating slowly. Bump \`Max output tokens\` down to 4000.
- **Stream chunks flowing then Abort** → just bump \`Timeout (ms)\`.
- **HTTP 4xx/5xx instead of 200** → bad request body / model name / format. Read the snippet in the FAIL message.

## BRAT
Standard update. Toggle is off until you turn it on.
releases.name: 0.1.31 — Verbose AI debug notifications for diagnosing Tailscale timeouts

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
Smart-story timeouts on a Tailscale-reached Ollama. Tailscale / VPN / load balancers close idle HTTP connections after 30-60 s; the model takes 60-180 s to generate, the connection sits idle, gets killed before tokens arrive.

## Fix
- New \`ai.stream\` (default true). Sends \`stream: true\` to the OpenAI-compat endpoint and parses Server-Sent Events. Each token arrives in a \`data: {…}\\n\\n\` chunk — connection never sits idle, Tailscale's idle timer never fires.
- Stream path uses native \`fetch\` + \`AbortController\`. Falls back to the existing requestUrl path on mobile or when the user disables streaming.
- Console log: \`[CCI AI] stream done. finish_reason: ... content length: ...\`. Easier diagnosis next time.

## BRAT
Standard update. \`Stream responses (SSE)\` toggle is in Settings → AI provider.
releases.name: 0.1.30 — SSE streaming so Tailscale / VPN idle timeouts don't kill the request

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
Smart story kept timing out despite \`Timeout (ms)\` set to 600000. Root cause: Obsidian's \`requestUrl\` has an undocumented internal timeout (~120 s on Electron desktop) that fires before our 0.1.28 Promise.race wrapper can — the underlying TCP request is cancelled by the OS layer regardless of our setting.

## Fix
- **Desktop**: native \`fetch\` + \`AbortController\`. The controller fires \`abort()\` after exactly \`timeoutMs\`. Your slider value is now the real ceiling.
- **Mobile**: stays on \`requestUrl\` + Promise.race timeout (mobile fetch is more locked down).
- AbortError → same friendly "AI request timed out after Xs" message.
- Response normalised to \`{ status, text }\` so downstream is platform-agnostic.

## BRAT
Standard update. Existing \`Timeout (ms)\` setting preserved.
releases.name: 0.1.29 — Desktop uses native fetch so timeoutMs is the real ceiling

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
- \`ai.timeoutMs\` default 60_000 → **300_000** (5 min). Local 35B MLX models routinely take 1-3 min for a 400-char story even with \`/no_think\` — the old 60 s cap cut requests mid-prompt.
- \`ai.maxRepairIterations\` default 2 → **1**. Each repair pass = another full round-trip; on a slow local model, 2 retries can compound to 9-15 min worst case before the user sees an error.
- \`timeoutMs\` was previously dead code (Obsidian's \`requestUrl\` has no built-in timeout option). \`AiProviderService.tryRequest\` now wraps the request in a \`Promise.race\` against a \`setTimeout\` rejection — clear error after the configured timeout instead of a silent hang.
- **Unchanged**: model name and baseUrl (per request — environment-specific).

## BRAT
Standard update. If you already set \`timeoutMs\` and \`maxRepairIterations\` manually, your values are preserved (settings merge keeps user overrides).
releases.name: 0.1.28 — Ollama-friendly defaults + enforced request timeout

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Diagnosed via your curl Test 1
\`usage.completion_tokens == 1327\` for a 20-char Chinese sentence — qwen3 emitted ~2500 tokens of reasoning before the answer. For the real Smart-story request that meant: thinking exhausted the 3000-token cap from 0.1.24, content arrived empty, parseStory threw with an empty snippet. The response shape also exposes \`choices[0].message.reasoning\` as a separate field — useful for diagnosing in the future.

## Changes

- **\`ai.maxOutputTokens\`** default 3000 → 8000.
- **NEW \`ai.suppressThinking\`** (default \`true\`) appends \`/no_think\` to the system prompt. qwen3 / DeepSeek-R1 / similar recognise the directive and skip the trace; no-op for other models.
- **NEW \`ai.responseFormat\`** (default \`json_object\`). Was hard-coded \`json_schema strict: true\` — OpenAI + Ollama ≥ 0.5.7 honour it, MLX builds are finicky. \`json_object\` is universally supported. User can swap to \`json_schema\` or \`none\` in Settings → AI provider.
- **Diagnostic empty-completion errors** — if \`finish_reason == "length"\` the message tells you to bump max tokens; if \`reasoning\` is populated but content empty, it tells you to enable Suppress thinking.

## Curl-test verification you can run after upgrading
\`\`\`bash
curl -sS http://localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions \\
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \\
  -d '{
    "model":"qwen3.6:35b-mlx",
    "messages":[
      {"role":"system","content":"You write Chinese.\n/no_think"},
      {"role":"user","content":"Write 100 chars Chinese about a cat."}
    ],
    "response_format":{"type":"json_object"},
    "max_tokens":8000
  }'
\`\`\`
Compare \`usage.completion_tokens\` with vs without \`/no_think\` — should drop sharply.

## BRAT
Standard update.
releases.name: 0.1.27 — qwen3 thinking-model support + json_object default

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
0.1.24's three-tier JSON fallback couldn't recover stories that were never delivered — the user's Notice showed an empty \`Raw snippet:\` line, which means the LLM response body was actually empty or had an unrecognized envelope and 0.1.24's \`extractText\` returned \`JSON.stringify(json)\` (i.e. \`"{}"\`).

## Diagnostics
- \`chatJson\` now \`console.log\`s the POST URL + body and the HTTP status + raw response.text. Open DevTools / use a mobile-debug tool and paste the log when something looks off.

## Empty-response detection
- Explicit errors for empty bodies, non-JSON envelopes, and empty completions, with hints at \`max_tokens\` and the connection test.

## extractText broader coverage
Now handles:
- \`choices[0].message.content\` (string OR array form)
- \`choices[0].text\` (legacy completions)
- \`output_text\` (Responses API shortcut)
- \`output[…].content\` (Responses API)
- Ollama native: \`/api/chat\` \`message.content\` + \`/api/generate\` \`response\`
- Falls back to \`""\` (not \`JSON.stringify\`) so unrecognized envelopes surface the explicit empty-completion error.

## BRAT
Standard update. If story generation still fails after this release, please copy the relevant \`[CCI AI]\` lines from the console — the actual HTTP status + raw response text will pinpoint the issue exactly.
releases.name: 0.1.26 — Diagnose empty / non-OpenAI LLM responses

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
Settings → Data → "Open stats" used to leave the user looking at the Settings modal while Stats opened invisibly behind it. Now:
- Closes the Settings modal via `app.setting.close()` (best-effort).
- Calls `revealLeaf` AND `setActiveLeaf(leaf, { focus: true })` on the new/existing Stats leaf so the tab comes to the front with focus.

## BRAT
Standard update.
releases.name: 0.1.25 — Focus Stats leaf when opened from Settings

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Story JSON recovery
Three-tier waterfall in `parseStory`:
1. Strict `JSON.parse` on the outermost `{…}` slice.
2. Regex-salvage just the `"textChinese"` field (from 0.1.23).
3. **NEW** — `longestCjkRun()` picks the longest contiguous run of CJK + Chinese / ASCII punctuation + whitespace in the raw response. Triggers when the provider returned plain Chinese prose without any JSON wrapping (Ollama OpenAI-compat with `response_format: json_schema` is known to ignore the flag and stream raw text).
4. Each fallback surfaces a Notice so the user knows the model didn't comply and quality may be lower.
5. `console.log("[CCI Story] raw LLM response:", raw)` fires on every call — DevTools shows what came back regardless of which tier resolved.

Prompt also gained: *"Return ONLY a single JSON object. No prose before or after. No markdown code fences."*

## Open-as-Markdown toolbar button
New `file-text` icon between the display-mode select and the overflow `…` in the Chinese view toolbar. Click → same leaf swaps to Obsidian's built-in Markdown editor on the same file. Round-trip back is the existing ribbon icon.

## BRAT
Standard update.
releases.name: 0.1.24 — Robust LLM JSON recovery + Open-as-Markdown toolbar button

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
Two bug fixes.

## Flashcards: card now advances after classification
Classifying a word in **Due** mode (Known / Partial / Unknown) didn't update `srs.dueAt`, so the next render still found the same word at the top of the due queue and the same card kept reappearing. **Unclassified** had the same risk in edge cases.

Fix:
- `applyTriage` and the Partial-axes Save handler now snapshot the marked record's key BEFORE the mutation, call `setStatus` / `setAxes`, then also call `srs.applyGrade` (`good` for Known, `hard` for Partial, `again` for Unknown — `ignored` skips grading). This pushes `srs.dueAt` so Due mode actually drops the word.
- New shared `flashcardsQueue(mode)` is the single source of truth for the renderer, Skip, and the new `advancePastIfPresent(markedKey)` helper — which advances `triageIndex` only when the marked record is still in the post-mutation queue.

## Story generation: "JSON Parse error: Unexpected EOF"
The model's reply was truncated by `maxOutputTokens` (default was 1500, too tight for a 400-char Chinese story plus JSON overhead).

Fix:
- Bumped `ai.maxOutputTokens` default to 3000.
- `parseStory` is now defensive:
  - Tighter code-fence regex.
  - Slices to the outermost `{ … }` so leading thinking / trailing chatter is dropped.
  - On `JSON.parse` failure, regex-salvages the `"textChinese"` value alone so the story is still readable even when the glossary/target lists got cut. Salvaged stories ship with empty glossary/target arrays — already handled downstream.
  - Error messages now include a 240-char snippet for diagnosis.

## BRAT
Standard update.
releases.name: 0.1.23 — Flashcards advance + robust story JSON parsing

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

### Monthly view bars finally render
`recentBucketLabels` for the month bucket was constructing first-of-month dates in **local time** (`new Date(yyyy, mm, 1)`) and then converting via `.toISOString()` (UTC). In any positive-UTC timezone the label rolled back into the previous month, so labels never matched the YYYY-MM keys produced by `bucketTimestamps`. Now built via `Date.UTC(...)`.

### Dashboard no longer scrolls horizontally
The per-note exposure table was a direct child of `.cci-dash-notes` with no horizontal-overflow wrapper, so long note paths pushed the dashboard wider than the viewport. The table now lives inside `.cci-dash-notes-tablewrap` (`overflow-x: auto`); the dashboard itself stays fixed-width.

## Triage → Flashcards
The Triage tab is now **Flashcards**. New 3-button mode selector:

1. **Unclassified** — original Triage flow (most-frequent `status === "new"` records).
2. **Due** — same card UI, queue = `SrsScheduler.due()` sorted by `srs.dueAt`.
3. **Smart story** — LLM-generated story over today's due words.

Mode is remembered in settings.

## Smart story flashcards
- Greyed-out unless `ai.enabled` AND `testConnection()` succeeds. Failure shows the path to Settings → AI provider.
- Once enabled, panel shows current params (due count, target HSK auto, length, known-coverage threshold, max repair iterations) and a Generate button.
- On click: `StoryGenerator.generatePreview()` runs the prompt + `validateStory` + repair loop (bounded by `ai.maxRepairIterations`), writes the validated story to `${story.folder}/.cci-flashcards-preview.md`, and opens it in the Chinese view.
- The preview is **not** a permanent note. Action row: Open / Save as note (renames to slug) / Generate again (overwrites with a new due-words pick) / Discard (deletes).
- Existing "Generate Review Story" modal still works — `generateAndSave` is now a thin wrapper over `generatePreview` + `commitPreviewAsNote`.

## BRAT
Standard update. 22 tests pass.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
Addresses the issues from the latest screenshot + asks.

## Stats layout
`.cci-stats` was scrolling horizontally — the dashboard cards and Scope dropdown were running off the visible area on iPhone widths. Now:
- `overflow-x: hidden` on the main container; `overflow-y: auto` for vertical scroll only.
- Header is `flex-wrap: wrap`; title shrinks with ellipsis; scope dropdown sized to fit the remaining row width.
- Per-note exposure table is the ONLY thing allowed to horizontally scroll (its own `.cci-stats-tablewrap` keeps the existing `overflow-x: auto`).

## Progress chart filter
5 checkboxes above the chart: Tracked / Classified / Known / Partial / Unknown. Each series toggles independently. Default = Classified + Known (the most useful pair for a learner in active triage). Works for both bars and cumulative-area modes.

## HSK coverage chart (new)
New section under Progress: 6 horizontal stacked bars, one per HSK 1-6 level, showing what fraction of each level falls in each status bucket. Bucket filter at top: Known / Partial / Unknown / New / Untracked. Defaults to Known only (per request). Hover a segment for exact counts. Level totals come from the bundled HSK 2.0 word list (~150 / ~150 / ~300 / ~600 / ~1300 / ~2500).

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
Addresses three issues from 0.1.19:

## Comfort level — always global
The card was reading the scoped records, so it changed when the user picked a per-note scope. Comfort level is a learner-wide metric, so it now always reads from the full vocabulary and the hint says so explicitly. All other cards + the per-note table still follow the scope dropdown.

## Today's bar finally shows
Root cause: `firstSeenAt` only fires on a record's first sighting, so users classifying already-tracked records today saw no bar (their words were tracked yesterday by the vault index).

New `classifiedAt` schema field is stamped the first time a record transitions out of `status === "new"` (in `setStatus`, `setAxes`, and the batch `markAllNewAs`). Backfilled in `dedupeOnLoad` to `updatedAt` for pre-existing non-new records.

The Progress chart's first series switched from "Tracked added" → "Classified", which is the metric the triage flow actually grows. The Learned series still uses `knownAt`.

## Area chart option
New "Cumulative" / "Per period" selector. Cumulative renders a running-total area curve — motivating for a learner watching their total grow over time. Per-period keeps the grouped bars. Default is cumulative.

## Triage Partial axes
Was hard-coded to `pinyinKnownMeaningUnknown`. Now "Partial…" opens an inline 3-checkbox editor (Characters / Pinyin / Meaning) + Save / Cancel. Save calls `setAxes`; status derives via existing `statusFromAxes`.
