releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
After 0.1.18 backfilled HSK 1-6 onto thousands of vault-indexed records, several dashboard behaviours became unhelpful and one new feature was needed. This release addresses all six in one pass.

## Fixes

### HSK Comfort level
Now skips `ignored` always and `new` when `statsExcludeNew` is on (default). Pre-backfill the denominator was sparse (~30 seed records); post-backfill it ballooned with auto-indexed "new" words and the card fell to "n/a".

### Default scope = "all vocabulary"
`openStatsView()` no longer scopes to the active note. The per-note toolbar badge calls a new explicit `openStatsForNote(notePath)`, so the per-note view is still one tap away.

### Live updates
`markWord` / `markWordIgnored` and the new batch op now call `refreshStatsViews()`. The dashboard, comfort level, and progress chart update without re-opening the view. This is also why today's bar in the chart wasn't appearing — the view was stale.

### Exclude-new toggle
Dashboard header has a checkbox: percentages compute against `known + partial + unknown` instead of `total - ignored`. Persisted via `statsExcludeNew` setting. Per-note table reuses the same rule.

### Batch "Mark all N new words as Unknown"
New `VocabularyStore.markAllNewAs(status)`. Dashboard button on global scope when there are still new words. One `confirm()` before applying.

## New: Triage tab
Third tab on the stats view. Picks the most-frequent records still in status === "new", presents one card at a time:

- Large term
- Pinyin + HSK level + seen count + first 2 definitions
- **Example sentence pulled from a note the word was actually seen in** (Chinese sentence boundaries honoured: `。！？!?;；…`), with the term highlighted and a clickable link back to the source note.
- Four action buttons: ✓ Known / ? Partial / ✗ Unknown / Ignore
- Skip → button to cycle without marking

The queue rebuilds after each tap, so the next-most-frequent unclassified word slots in automatically. Lets you blast through hundreds of words in a few minutes without having to open and read every note.

## BRAT
Standard update.
releases.name: 0.1.19 — Stats UX after HSK backfill + Triage tab

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
Extends HSK level coverage from the sparse hand-curated seed (mostly HSK 1-3) to **all six HSK 2.0 levels** for ~5000 simplified terms, without any schema change.

## How
- Build-time script `scripts/build-hsk-map.mjs` fetches the official HSK 2.0 lists from [glxxyz/hskhsk.com](https://github.com/glxxyz/hskhsk.com) (MIT, © 2020 Alan Davies) and emits `src/dictionary/hskMap.generated.ts` — a compact `{ "term": level }` mapping. The generated file is checked in; the runtime makes no network call.
- `DictionaryService.index(entry)` backfills `entry.hsk` from the map when the entry has none. Seed entries with explicit HSK metadata are NOT overwritten — existing HSK 1-3 data stays authoritative.
- `VocabularyStore.dedupeOnLoad()` does the same backfill for records already in the user's vocab store, so previously-tracked HSK 4-6 words pick up their level on next plugin load.

## What hskhsk provides
- Only the simplified-term → HSK-level (1–6) mapping is imported.
- No definitions, pinyin, examples, audio, traditional variants, or frequency data are taken from this source.
- CC-CEDICT remains the authoritative source for definitions / pinyin.

## Behaviour
- Stats `Dashboard` "Comfort level" now climbs past HSK 3 once HSK 4-6 words are marked known.
- Stats `Words` tab HSK filter (4 / 5 / 6) returns matching records.
- `WordPopup` `HSK: 4` line lights up for any HSK 4-6 known word in any note.

## Conflict rules
1. Existing HSK 1-3 metadata wins.
2. hskhsk fills missing HSK 1-3.
3. hskhsk is primary for HSK 4-6.
4. Lowest level wins on duplicates.

## Attribution
NOTICE.md updated. License: MIT, © 2020 Alan Davies. Data path: `data/lists/HSK Official 2012 L{1..6}.txt`.

## BRAT
Standard update. On the first load after upgrading, `dedupeOnLoad()` backfills HSK metadata across existing records (one debounced save).
releases.name: 0.1.18 — HSK 1-6 categorization via hskhsk.com import

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
This is a structural release: previous fixes for the persistent bugs treated symptoms (multi-attempt dispatch, scroll math after editor rebuild, per-line heading detection). 0.1.17 fixes them at the source.

## Init paint
Eliminates the async race that caused "open note → no annotations until I switch the mode". A new module-level `tokenCache` is filled by `tokenize()` and read **synchronously** by the decoration ViewPlugin's constructor. `onOpen` pre-warms the cache *before* creating the editor, so the first paint already has tokens.

## Scroll preservation
The display-mode toggle no longer destroys the editor. The toolbar handler captures the topmost-visible doc offset, applies CSS + `redecorate()`, then `scrollIntoView`s the same offset on the next frame. Same logical line stays near the top across Color ↔ 2-line ↔ 3-line.

## Heading widget
Detection regex widened to allow up to 3 leading spaces (CommonMark). With init paint fixed, every build runs against a measured viewport, so all heading tokens render consistently as headings in 2/3-line modes.

## Vocab dedup
- `bySurface()` resolves the canonical `simplified|pinyin` key via the dictionary — same word in any note lands on the same record.
- `ensure()` accumulates the visible surface into `record.surfaces[]` on every call.
- A one-time `dedupeOnLoad()` re-keys legacy records and merges duplicates from existing vaults.

## Vault indexing (NEW)
On first plugin load (or via Settings → Data → "Index vault"), the plugin walks every Markdown file in the vault, tokenizes the Chinese spans, and records each word — so the dashboard reflects the *whole* vault, not only notes you have opened. Chunked + yielding so the UI stays responsive.

## Dictionary auto-download (NEW)
On first onload, if `.cci-dictionary.json` is missing, the plugin silently downloads CC-CEDICT in the background. Toggleable in Settings → Data.

## Progress graph (NEW)
Dashboard gains a Progress section with a Day / Week / Month selector and two SVG bar series:
- "Tracked added" — bucketed `firstSeenAt`
- "Learned" — bucketed `knownAt` (new schema field stamped the first time a word reaches `known`; backfilled for existing known records using `updatedAt`).

## BRAT
Standard update. Expect a "Downloading dictionary…" notice (if applicable) and then an "Indexing vault…" notice on the first run after upgrading.
releases.name: 0.1.17 — fix init paint via sync token cache, no-rebuild mode switch, dedup, vault index, progress graph

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Mode-switch scroll position
Switching from Color to 2-line / 3-line used to jump to the top because the saved pixel `scrollTop` maps to a different logical line under the new line-height. Now captures the doc offset of the viewport-top line and `scrollIntoView`s it after the rebuild — same line stays approximately at top.

## Dashboard
Stats Dashboard's class names had no CSS rules, so cards rendered as bare blocks. This release adds the missing styles: grid of stat cards (with accent borders for known / partial / unknown / new), tab styling, per-note table, and tinted Words-table rows / status cells.

## Init paint
"Open note → must switch the mode for annotations to render" — the single rAF dispatch from 0.1.15 still raced Obsidian's view-attach on some opens. Now dispatches at rAF, rAF+rAF, and +120ms; each one hits the cached-token fast path so cost is negligible.

## Chinese heading in 2-line / 3-line modes
The `RubyWidget` replaces the original text, which drops the heading's font-size context. The pipeline now precomputes the heading level for each line (cached per line) and passes it into the widget; the widget tags itself `cci-stack-h{N}` and the chars row scales to match the heading size and weight.

## BRAT
Standard update.
releases.name: 0.1.16 — mode-switch scroll, dashboard CSS, init paint retry, heading widget

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Frontmatter hide (new approach)
Previous attempts (0.1.13 line decorations, 0.1.14 atomic replace) tried to hide the YAML inside CodeMirror — both broke its layout / measurement pipeline. 0.1.15 instead **strips the `---` … `---` block at the TextFileView boundary**:
- Frontmatter is cached on the view in memory and never reaches the CodeMirror document.
- On save, it is re-prefixed by `getViewData()` so the file on disk is unchanged.
- Body editing in the Chinese view works normally. (YAML is not editable from this view — edit it in the normal Markdown view.)

## Init paint
"Open note → no annotations until I switch the display mode" — the redecorate transaction dispatched after the async tokenize was racing CM6's first measure pass. Now deferred to `requestAnimationFrame`, so the freshly-built decorations always paint on first open.

## Scroll-up lag
`update()` was re-stringifying and re-hashing the entire document on every viewport change. New fast path: when the doc hasn't changed and tokens are already cached, the decoration set is rebuilt against the new viewport directly — no doc.toString / hash. Long notes should scroll-up smoothly now.

## BRAT
Standard update.
releases.name: 0.1.15 — YAML hide via view boundary, init-paint fix, scroll-up fix

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fix
0.1.13 broke colors / 2-line / 3-line / clicks on notes that start with a `---` frontmatter block. Root cause: `display: none` on `.cm-line` desyncs CodeMirror's line-height / position cache, after which downstream decorations silently fail to render.

## Change
Frontmatter is now hidden with one atomic `Decoration.replace({})` covering the whole `---` … `---` block (single decoration source, no separate ViewPlugin, no CSS line-hiding). CM6 collapses the range natively — layout / measurement stays coherent — and the Chinese decoration pipeline continues to render correctly below the block.

## BRAT
Standard BRAT update.
releases.name: 0.1.14 — hide YAML frontmatter without breaking CM6 layout

Drops the separately-mounted `wikilinkPlugin` / `frontmatterPlugin` and reverts the `reconfigureEditor()` → `redecorate()` switch on the display-mode toggle.

## New

- **Known% badge** in the toolbar, just after the kebab button. Shows what % of the current note's recognised Chinese words you've marked as fully known. Color-coded (green ≥80%, yellow ≥50%, red below). Tooltip breaks down partial / unknown / new. Tap → stats view scoped to this note. Recomputes on every doc change or mark.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Colors didn't load on first open** — the async decoration pass dispatched an empty transaction; CM6 treated it as a no-op and skipped repaint. Now uses a real redecorate effect so annotations appear on first paint.
- **Word stuck red after mark unknown → checkbox edit** — `RubyWidget.eq()` was comparing the live in-place-mutated WordRecord on both sides, so the widget was never refreshed. Now captures color/axes/pinyin/gloss as primitives at widget construction.
- **3-line: long English gloss overlapped neighbors** — gloss is now a normal block child of the inline-block stack instead of an absolute overlay, so when the gloss is wider than the chars, the whole word naturally takes more horizontal space and neighboring words are pushed apart.
- **3-line: pinyin and gloss touched** — vertical gap between them increased.
- **Line numbers removed** — wasted horizontal space on phones; not needed for reading.

BRAT → Check for updates.
releases.name: 0.1.6 — paint, axes update, 3-line spacing

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fix

Pinyin for multi-syllable words (eg 想象 → xiǎng xiàng) was drawn as one centered string above the whole word, causing wide pinyin to bleed sideways and break horizontal alignment with neighboring words.

Now each Chinese character has its own cell with its own pinyin syllable absolutely positioned above it — each syllable lines up over its character. Characters render inline at the normal text baseline, so annotated and unannotated chars share one baseline on the same line.

Fallback: if syllable count doesn't match character count (rare CC-CEDICT entries), word-level pinyin row is shown with ellipsis truncation.

BRAT → Check for updates.
releases.name: 0.1.5 — per-character pinyin alignment

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Knowledge model (breaking-feel — recommend resetting plugin data)

Word knowledge is now three independent axes: **characters / pinyin / translation**. The popup shows three checkboxes; status and color are derived.

- **Green** = all three known
- **Yellow** = some axes known
- **Red** = none known
- **Blue** = not marked yet

Inline annotation only shows the axes you don't already know. Marking modes:
- `Known` mode → tap sets all three true
- `Unknown` mode → tap sets all three false
- `Partial` mode → tap opens the popup with the three checkboxes pre-filled

## Fixes

- **Edit mode no longer breaks**: in Edit mode, replacement widgets (ruby / 3-line stack) are suppressed. Only coloring is applied so typing and the cursor work normally.
- **Baseline alignment**: 2-line / 3-line now set a fixed `line-height` on the editor so unannotated CJK characters share the same baseline slot as annotated ones — no more height jitter on lines that mix annotated and plain text.
- **Banner doesn't shift scroll**: toolbar reserves the banner row even when empty, so toggling Mark mode doesn't lose your reading position.

## New

- **Per-note stats**: `Stats` now has a scope dropdown — "All vocabulary" or any note that has recorded exposures. Opening Stats from a Chinese note defaults to that note.

## Reset data
Settings → Data → "Reset plugin data" removes old records. Recommended after this update because the data model added the `axes` and per-note counters.

BRAT → Check for updates.
releases.name: 0.1.4 — knowledge axes, 3 colors, edit-safe, per-note stats

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Tap pops keyboard + popup**: in Read and Mark modes, tapping a word now blocks the editor from claiming focus. No caret jump, no mobile keyboard. Edit mode keeps normal CM6 cursor placement; long-press still opens the popup everywhere.
- **3-line layout broken**: native `<ruby>` puts a second `<rt>` *beside* the first in WebKit/Chromium. Replaced the 3-line widget with a manual vertical stack (gloss → pinyin → chars). 2-line still uses native ruby.
- **Long-press false positives**: tracks pointer movement and cancels long-press on scroll/drag.

## New

- **Reader font size slider** (14–40 px). Quick access in the toolbar overflow menu while reading; also in plugin settings. Applies to the Chinese Learning View only.

BRAT → Check for updates.
releases.name: 0.1.3 — fix tap/keyboard, 3-line layout, font slider

## UX cleanup

### Toolbar
- Dropped the explicit "Read" button. Read mode is the implicit default — Edit / Mark known / Mark unknown are icon toggles, all off = Read.
- Color toggles, stats, and "Generate story" moved into an overflow menu (kebab button).
- Single row that fits a phone screen.

### Fixes
- **Three-line mode**: was silently doing nothing for fresh notes because `new` words were skipped by the ruby gate. Now any non-known/non-ignored word annotates inline when 2-line / 3-line is selected.
- **Scroll jump on mark**: marking a word no longer rebuilds the editor. New CodeMirror state effect re-runs decorations against cached tokens, preserving scroll position and cursor.

### New word status
- `Spoken ✓, chars ?` — for when seeing pinyin is enough to recognize meaning but the characters themselves don't click yet. Wired through decorations, popup, stats filter, SRS.

### Stats view
- Explicit `← Back` button.
- Controls fit in one row.
- Table wrapped in a horizontal-scroll container so "Last seen" / "Due" columns no longer fall off-screen.

BRAT users: Check for updates.

releases.author: davadev
releases.description:
First BRAT-installable build of **Chinese Comprehensible Input** for Obsidian.

## Install via BRAT
1. Install the BRAT plugin in Obsidian.
2. BRAT → "Add Beta plugin" → paste `davadev/obsidian_chinese_comprehensible_input`.
3. Enable in Community Plugins.

## What's in V1
- Editable Chinese Learning View built on `TextFileView` + CodeMirror 6.
- Dictionary-aware lattice tokenizer with HSK overlay.
- One-click CC-CEDICT download from MDBG in plugin settings (mobile-safe, uses `requestUrl` + `DecompressionStream`).
- Word statuses, exposure tracking with daily timestamps, SM-2-like SRS.
- Optional OpenAI-compatible AI story generation with JSON-schema validation and repair loop (Ollama-compatible out of the box).
- Stats view with per-word daily exposure graph and learner-HSK estimate.
- Desktop + iOS + Android compatible (`isDesktopOnly: false`, no Node/Electron at runtime).

See `NOTICE.md` for CC-CEDICT (CC BY-SA 4.0) and HSK attribution.
releases.name: 0.1.0 — first BRAT-installable build
